title: City of Syracuse Announces Water Department Public Meeting on April 4
summary: Syracuse Water Department will host a public information meeting on April 4, 2024 at Nottingham High School to inform residents about the Brookford Pump Station project. The project will replace water mains and lead-containing service lines at no cost to property owners in the Meadowbrook neighborhood, affecting approximately 750 homes across multiple streets.

political_signal.description: Public infrastructure investment in water quality and lead remediation reflects alignment with public health and environmental equity priorities. Community engagement meeting demonstrates transparency and constituent outreach.

processing_notes: Author listed as 'Unknown' in source metadata
Sharon F. Owens mentioned as Mayor in header navigation but not in article body
Mechanical extraction incorrectly flagged 'Syracuse Fire Department' - article references fire protection as benefit of project, not department involvement
No dollar amounts provided in release
Meeting is recurring program element (long-term infrastructure plan referenced)
Lead remediation at no cost to residents is key constituent benefit worth flagging for CIO infrastructure planning context
